compiled_state.title: Roll out JSON Schemas for all current JSON outputs (+ fix MCP validate signature)
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
Generalize the JSON-Schema prototype into authoritative schemas for rebar's
CURRENT JSON outputs, used as a cross-interface drift oracle and as MCP
outputSchemas. (Child of the "--json everywhere" epic.) Scope below is the
result of a full codebase audit.

## Already in place (prototype — extend, don't redo)
- src/rebar/schemas/ticket_state.schema.json + schemas/__init__.py loader
- tests/interfaces/test_schema_ticket_state.py (validates show via lib/CLI/MCP)
- MCP show_ticket returns typed TicketStateOut -> advertises outputSchema
- jsonschema in [dev] extra

## Distinct JSON shapes today (each needs a schema)
1. TicketState (object) — show; also array items of list, search. (DONE: schema exists)
2. TicketState-LLM (renamed short keys) — show/list/ready --format=llm
3. DepsGraph — deps: {ticket_id,deps[],blockers[],children[],ready_to_work}
4. NextBatch — next-batch --json: {epic_id,epic_title,batch_size,available_pool,batch[{id,title,priority,type,files[],files_likely_read[]}],skipped_*[]}
5. ListDescendants — {epics[],stories[],tasks[],bugs[],parents_with_children[]}
6. ClarityResult — clarity-check: {score,verdict,threshold(,passed)}
7. ValidateReport — validate --json: {score,critical_issues[],major_issues[],minor_issues[],warnings[],suggestions[]}
8. BridgeStatus — bridge-status --format=json (raw .bridge-status.json + unresolved_alerts_count)
9. FileImpact[] — get-file-impact: [{path,reason}]
10. VerifyCommands[] — get-verify-commands: [{dd_id,dd_text,command}]
11. Scratch envelopes — scratch get/set/clear: {status:"ok"|"hit"|"miss"|"error",...}
12. ErrorEnvelope (show not-found) — {error,input,message}
13. BridgeFsck findings (currently text-only; dict exists) — {orphaned[],duplicates[],stale[]}

## Consolidation (use shared $ref sub-schemas — author once, reference everywhere)
- TicketState is the keystone: list/search are {type:array,items:{$ref:TicketState}} (byte-identical reducer output).
- Promote reused sub-objects to named $refs: comment {body,author,timestamp}; dep {target_id,relation,link_uuid} (ALSO used by DepsGraph.deps[] — same entry); file_impact_entry {path,reason} (shared by get-file-impact AND TicketState.file_impact[]); verify_command_entry {dd_id,dd_text,command} (shared by get-verify-commands AND TicketState.verify_commands[]); bridge_alert; revert.
- next-batch batch/skipped_* items all share a BatchItem base {id,title} (+ variant fields conflict_file/conflict_with, blocked_story) -> 3 variants, all $ref.
- Unify result envelopes: CheckResult/GateResult {verdict|passed, score?, threshold?, metrics?, reason?} shared by clarity-check + (json-ified) check-ac + quality-check. ClarityResult is a subset.
- Unify issue reports: IssueReport {score?, issues:[{severity,kind,ticket_id?,detail}], counts} shared by validate (collapse the 5 severity arrays), fsck, bridge-fsck.
- Unify ErrorEnvelope: show not-found {error,input,message} vs scratch {status:"error",code,reason} -> one {status:"error",code,message} referenced wherever a structured error is emitted on stdout.

## MCP typed returns (advertise outputSchema; ~12 models, several shared)
Follow the show_ticket/TicketStateOut pattern for every read tool returning bare
dict/list/object. One model per shape; reuse across tools:
- TicketStateOut -> list_tickets, search (DONE for show_ticket)
- DepsGraphOut -> ticket_deps;  NextBatchOut -> next_batch;  ReadyOut -> ready_tickets (after Bug 2)
- ClarityResultOut -> clarity_check;  GateResultOut(passed,output) -> check_ac + quality_check
- ValidateReportOut -> validate (after Bug 1);  FileImpactItemOut -> get_file_impact (+ types set_file_impact input)
- VerifyCommandItemOut -> get_verify_commands (+ types set_verify_commands input)
- StatusResultOut(id,status,assignee?) -> transition_ticket + claim_ticket + reopen_ticket
- CreateResultOut(id,alias) -> create_ticket (resolve Mismatch C);  AckOut (optional) for the 10 "ok" writers

## Prerequisite bug fixes (these tools can't be typed until they return correct data)
- BUG 1 (validate arg): MCP validate (mcp_server.py:154) AND library validate (__init__.py:197) pass ticket_id, but engine validate is repo-wide (validate-issues.sh:80-83 rejects positionals). Confirmed: returns {output:"Unknown option: <id>",exit_code:1}. Fix: drop ticket_id in both layers; call ["validate","--json"].
- BUG 2 (ready --json): library ready() (__init__.py:316) calls ["ready","--json"], but engine ready only has --format {ids,llm}/--epic. Confirmed: rebar.ready() raises RebarError(exit 2) -> MCP ready_tickets ALWAYS errors. Fix: call `ready --format llm` (or add --json to the engine) and define ReadyOut.

## Other interface mismatches to reconcile
- C: create_ticket — MCP returns bare id; library can return {id,alias}; agent workflow favors {id,alias}. Standardize.
- D: edit_ticket — MCP lacks `ticket_type` param (library/CLI have it). Add it.
- E: set_file_impact/set_verify_commands — MCP params typed bare `list` (no inputSchema); pair with item models.
- F: fsck(recover:bool) maps to two CLI subcommands (fsck / fsck-recover) — cosmetic; document.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A JSON Schema authored under src/rebar/schemas/ for each shape #2-#13 (incl. shared $ref sub-schemas in a common file)
- [ ] Cross-interface conformance test per schema (lib/CLI/MCP as applicable), reusing the prototype pattern
- [ ] All read MCP tools advertise outputSchema via typed returns (grouped models above)
- [ ] BUG 1 fixed: validate takes no ticket_id (lib+MCP), matches repo-wide engine; test
- [ ] BUG 2 fixed: ready works end-to-end (lib+MCP return data, not an error); test
- [ ] Mismatches C/D/E reconciled across CLI/library/MCP (parity); F documented
- [ ] Schema source-of-truth decision documented (hand-authored vs generated from models) + drift guard if both coexist
- [ ] Docs link schemas from README/CLAUDE/docs/event-schema.md

comments.body:
VALIDATION (3 independent subagents, read-only + empirical). Both prerequisite
bugs REPRODUCED. Most shapes confirmed; the following CORRECTIONS to the plan
above are validated against current code and supersede the originals:

1. CRITICAL — TicketState is produced by TWO DIVERGENT reducers, not one.
   `rebar show` (and the not-found ErrorEnvelope) come from the jq reducer in
   ticket-lib-api.sh (ticket_show), NOT ticket-show.sh. `list`/`search`/native
   `reduce_ticket` use the Python reducer (ticket_reducer/). They are NOT
   byte-identical:
   - jq show: emits conditional `verify_commands`; OMITS `parent_status_uuid`.
   - Python (list/search): always emits `parent_status_uuid`; NEVER emits
     `verify_commands` (no VERIFY_COMMANDS processor exists in _processors.py).
   => The "list/search are just {array of $ref TicketState}" keystone is only
   true for the CORE fields. Action: either (a) make the TicketState schema cover
   BOTH (verify_commands AND parent_status_uuid both optional; additionalProperties
   already true), OR (b) add a PREREQUISITE task to UNIFY the two reducers so
   show/list/search are truly identical. This show-vs-list divergence is a latent
   parity issue worth its own bug (see comment to follow / ask maintainer).

2. `verify_commands` in TicketState is jq-show-only (also get-verify-commands).
   The shared verify_command_entry $ref holds for show/get-verify-commands but
   NOT for list/search/native. Same caveat as #1.

3. Add `parent_status_uuid` to the TicketState inventory (Python-path field).

4. NextBatch corrections: the three "awaiting/blocked" arrays
   (skipped_blocked_story, skipped_design_awaiting, skipped_manual_awaiting) ALL
   use the key `blocked_story` (not per-variant names) -> {id,title,blocked_story}.
   Plus a MISSED top-level variant: `next-batch --limit=0` emits
   {epic_id, batch_size:0, tasks:[]} (key `tasks`, not `batch`).

5. Synthetic library/MCP shapes that are NOT raw engine JSON (the typed models
   must match what the interfaces ACTUALLY return):
   - clarity_check: library/MCP add `passed` -> {score,verdict,threshold,passed}
     (engine --json is only {score,verdict,threshold}).
   - validate: library adds `exit_code` -> 7 keys (engine --json has 6).
   - check_ac / quality_check: library returns {passed, output} — NO engine JSON
     counterpart (wraps exit code + text). Model these as GateResultOut.
   - transition/claim/reopen: library SYNTHESIZES {id,status[,assignee]} client-side
     (engine emits no such JSON). StatusResultOut is a library/MCP shape, not engine.
   - All write tools return literal "ok" (MCP) vs None (library) — return-type skew.

6. Confirmed exactly as stated: DepsGraph keys; ListDescendants (lists of id
   strings); ClarityResult; ValidateReport (6 keys); scratch envelopes; the shared
   `dep` entry {target_id,relation,link_uuid} (DepsGraph.deps IS state["deps"]);
   file_impact_entry passthrough; the {id,title} skipped_* base. ErrorEnvelope
   `error` literal = "ticket_not_found". LLM KEY_MAP is duplicated (Python
   llm_format.py + a jq mirror in ticket-lib-api.sh); `conflicts->cf` is dead.

7. BUG 1 (validate) + BUG 2 (ready) REPRODUCED empirically:
   - validate <id> -> "Unknown option: <id>", exit 1; library validate() REQUIRES
     ticket_id (can't be called id-less -> TypeError), so neither path works.
   - ready --json -> argparse exit 2 "unrecognized arguments: --json"; rebar.ready()
     raises; MCP ready_tickets ALWAYS errors. Engine ready only has --format{ids,llm}.
   - Spot-check: no OTHER read function has a flag/arg mismatch (clarity/check_ac/
     quality_check/get_*/deps/next_batch/search/list/show all verified working).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
TARGETS (derived from audit inventory; to be confirmed). Story = schemas + typed MCP returns + 2 prerequisite bug fixes.

SCHEMAS (new files under src/rebar/schemas/, one per distinct shape #2-#13):
- common.schema.json (shared $ref sub-objects: comment, dep, file_impact_entry, verify_command_entry, bridge_alert, revert, error_envelope, gate_result)
- ticket_state_llm.schema.json (#2 show/list/ready --output llm; short keys)
- deps_graph.schema.json (#3)
- next_batch.schema.json (#4; incl --limit=0 {epic_id,batch_size:0,tasks:[]} variant + blocked_story skipped_* shape)
- list_descendants.schema.json (#5)
- clarity_result.schema.json (#6; lib/MCP add `passed`)
- validate_report.schema.json (#7; lib adds exit_code)
- bridge_status.schema.json (#8)
- file_impact.schema.json (#9; array of file_impact_entry)
- verify_commands.schema.json (#10; array of verify_command_entry)
- scratch_envelope.schema.json (#11)
- error_envelope.schema.json (#12; show not-found {error,input,message})
- bridge_fsck.schema.json (#13)
- register all names in src/rebar/schemas/__init__.py
EXTEND existing: ticket_state.schema.json — add optional parent_status_uuid (Python reducer path) so list/search validate too.

MCP typed returns (mcp_server.py) — outputSchema via pydantic models, following TicketStateOut:
- TicketStateOut -> list_tickets, search (currently list returns bare list[dict])
- DepsGraphOut, NextBatchOut, ReadyOut(list[TicketStateOut]), ClarityResultOut,
  GateResultOut(passed,output) -> check_ac+quality_check, ValidateReportOut,
  FileImpactItemOut, VerifyCommandItemOut, StatusResultOut -> transition/claim/reopen, CreateResultOut.

PREREQUISITE BUGS (VERIFY current state — may already be resolved by native-reads/DSO migration):
- BUG1 validate arg: __init__.py:197 already takes no ticket_id + calls validate (✓ likely fixed). MCP validate() no id (✓). CONFIRM end-to-end.
- BUG2 ready: library ready() now routes via _reads (in-process), not engine --json. CONFIRM rebar.ready() + MCP ready_tickets return data, not error. (Note: depends on T2 giving engine `ready --output json` for the CLI/native_read_parity tests.)
Mismatches: C create_ticket MCP returns bare id -> {id,alias}; D edit_ticket MCP add ticket_type param; E set_file_impact/set_verify_commands typed item models; F fsck recover doc.

TESTS: extend tests/interfaces/test_schema_ticket_state.py pattern -> one conformance test per schema; MCP outputSchema advertised assertions.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
TARGETS — VERIFICATION CORRECTIONS (Explore audit, empirical). MATERIAL:
* BUG1 (validate arg) and BUG2 (ready) are ALREADY FIXED by the native-reads/DSO migration. Empirically: rebar.validate() works id-less + returns 6-key report; rebar.ready() returns a list; CLI `ready --json` + `validate --json` both work; MCP ready_tickets/validate return data. => This story's "2 prerequisite bug fixes" are MOOT — scope them as VERIFY-ONLY (add regression tests under T1; no code fix needed). Net story scope shrinks to schemas + typed MCP returns.
* The show-vs-list reducer DIVERGENCE flagged in the older validation comment is RESOLVED: native reads unified the reducer, so show/list/search emit byte-identical TicketState. No `parent_status_uuid` in output; do NOT add it to the schema. ticket_state.schema.json needs NO extension for list/search.
* ValidateReport: library does NOT currently add exit_code (older note said it did) — engine --json + library both return the 6-key shape. Schema = 6 keys; no exit_code.
* MCP typed-return reality CONFIRMED: only show_ticket is typed; these 14 return bare dict/list/object and need models: list_tickets, search, ticket_deps, ready_tickets, next_batch, clarity_check, check_ac, quality_check, validate, get_file_impact, get_verify_commands, transition_ticket, claim_ticket, reopen_ticket, create_ticket (bare str -> CreateResultOut, Mismatch C).
* Registry pattern confirmed: schemas/__init__.py load()/path()/NAME consts; replicate test_schema_ticket_state.py per schema.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: DONE (commit 45c4ae4). 13 schemas authored (common.schema.json shared $defs + 12 per-shape, ticket_state refactored to $ref common); registry-backed validator() resolves cross-file refs; OUTPUT_SCHEMAS map added. Conformance test test_schema_outputs.py validates every shape's REAL output (lib+CLI). 10 stable read MCP tools now typed (outputSchema); mismatch D (edit ticket_type) + E (typed set_file_impact/set_verify_commands inputSchema) fixed. Lifecycle/gate typing deferred to T3/T4 (shapes change there). BUG1/BUG2 already fixed by native-reads migration (regression cover in test_ready_output.py/test_cli.py). Full suite 1291 passed, 2 xfailed.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
